body.u: Looking back over my life, all seventeen years of it. The most important years being when I was in the 5th and 4th year at the Sheppey comprehensive. Because it was what I had achieved during these two years that made the difference of getting the career I wanted or not getting it. It only seems like yesterday that I was sitting down taking my C.S.E Maths exam, I can remember doing boring K.M.P which gets you nowhere, and the teachers didn’t care if you did your lessons or not, there was just no disciplining. You could just walk out of school when you liked, and if you were stopped by a teacher you could just say you were going to the Doctors, they wouldn’t need a note.
